resources.title: Year 9 Social Studies: Treaty of Waitangi - Understanding Our Foundation
resources.subject: Social Studies
resources.description: Comprehensive exploration of Te Tiriti o Waitangi and its contemporary relevance

passes.specialization: Cultural Authenticity & Te Ao Māori Integration

culturalOpening.karakia: Kia mau ki tō tikanga Māori, kia eke panuku, kia eke tangaroa
culturalOpening.whakatōhea: He mihi ki ngā tupuna, he mihi ki te whenua o Mangakōtukutuku, he mihi ki a Ngāti Kahungunu. Ko tēnei ako, he taonga mō ā tātou tamariki.
culturalOpening.localConnection: Connecting Social Studies learning to our sacred Mangakōtukutuku environment and Ngāti Kahungunu wisdom traditions

practices: Pōwhiri protocols for welcoming new learning
protocols: Opening karakia to center learning intentions
Cultural safety protocols for inclusive participation

phrases: He aha tēnei? - What is this?
He aha ai? - Why is this so?
Kei te mārama au - I understand
Me pēhea? - How do we do this?
teReoIntegration.pronunciation: Pronunciation guides included with macron emphasis

whakapapaConnections.ancestralKnowledge: Connecting to tupuna wisdom in Social Studies
whakapapaConnections.genealogicalLearning: Whakapapa connections to land, people, and knowledge
whakapapaConnections.intergenerationalWisdom: Elder knowledge integration and contemporary application

projectBasedLearning.realWorldProblem: How can we honor Te Tiriti while addressing contemporary social justice issues?
projectBasedLearning.communityImpact: Students will create solutions that benefit the Mangakōtukutuku community
projectBasedLearning.studentAgency: Multiple pathway choices allowing student-driven inquiry
authenticProducts: Collaborative research project with real impact
Creative solution implemented in local environment
Teaching resource shared with other classes

designThinkingIntegration.empathize: Students interview community members and gather diverse perspectives
designThinkingIntegration.define: Collaborative problem definition using cultural frameworks
designThinkingIntegration.ideate: Creative solution generation honoring both innovation and tradition
designThinkingIntegration.prototype: Hands-on creation and testing with community feedback

studentLedInquiry.researchMethods: Multiple research approaches including oral history and traditional knowledge

multipleRepresentations.visual: Infographics, mind maps, cultural imagery, and artistic expression
multipleRepresentations.auditory: Storytelling, waiata, discussions, and oral presentations
multipleRepresentations.kinesthetic: Hands-on activities, movement, and experiential learning

flexibleEngagement.choiceBoards: Learning menu options honoring different interests and strengths
flexibleEngagement.culturalPathways: Māori, Pacific, and diverse cultural learning approaches
flexibleEngagement.supportLevels: Scaffolded support from whānau and tuakana-teina

actionExpression.collaborativeWork: Group projects and peer teaching opportunities

culturalAssessment.holisticMethods: Whole-person evaluation including spiritual and emotional growth
culturalAssessment.communityFeedback: Elder and community member input on learning
culturalAssessment.ceremonialRecognition: Cultural celebration of achievement and progress

growthMindset.formativeFeedback: Continuous, constructive, and culturally affirming feedback
growthMindset.celebrateProgress: Recognition of effort, improvement, and cultural connection
